title: Atropis unilateralis
intro: Atropis unilateralis, commonly known as wormwood, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its bitter and aromatic properties. The health benefits of Atropis unilateralis include its use as a digestive aid, anti-inflammatory agent, and antiparasitic treatment, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include thujone, borneol, and camphor, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Atropis unilateralis is commonly used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and essential oils, with wormwood tea being one of the most popular preparations, used to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions.
